title: Detect Start of Cycle in Linked List

content.problemStatement:
You are given a singly linked list with NNN nodes. Each node contains an integer value.

Your task is to find the **index of the node where a cycle begins**, if a cycle exists. If the linked list has no cycle, print −1-1−1.

A cycle occurs when a node's next pointer points to a **previous node** or **itself** in the list. The pospospos is the 0-based index of the node that the last node points to.

Function Declaration**Function Name**detectCycledetectCycledetectCycle — This function determines whether a cycle exists in a singly linked list and returns the index of the node where the cycle begins.

Parametersheadheadhead: A pointer to the **first node** of the singly linked list.


May be NULLNULLNULL if the list is empty.
Each node contains an integer value and a pointer to the next node.
Return ValueReturns an integer representing the **0-based index of the node** where the cycle starts.


If **no cycle** is present in the linked list, returns −1-1−1.
`The input and output formats provided below are only for testing with custom inputs.`

Constraints:
0≤N≤1040 \le N \le 10^{4}0≤N≤104
−105≤Node.val≤105-10^{5} \le \text{Node.val} \le 10^{5}−105≤Node.val≤105
−1≤pos<N-1 \le \text{pos} < N−1≤pos<N

Input Format

The first line contains an integer NNN — the number of nodes in the linked list.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers — the values of the nodes.
The third line contains an integer pospospos — the index that the last node connects to (−1-1−1 if no cycle).


Output Format

Print a single integer — the 0-based index of the node where the cycle begins.
If there is no cycle, print −1-1−1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Linked list: 11 -> 22 -> 33 -> 44 -> 55 -> 66 -> 77
The last node 77 points back to node at index 4 (55). Cycle starts at 55.

Explanation:
Linked list: 3 -> 6 -> 9 -> 12 -> 15
The last node 15 points to index 1 (6). Cycle starts at 6.

code.Python3:
# Function to detect the start index of the cycle in a linked list
def detectCycle(head):
    if not head:
        return -1

    slow = head
    fast = head

    # Step 1: Detect cycle using Floyd’s algorithm
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
        if slow == fast:
            break

    # If no cycle detected
    if not fast or not fast.next:
        return -1

    # Step 2: Find start of the cycle
    slow = head
    index = 0
    while slow != fast:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next
        index += 1

    return index
